title: Quiz: Property-Based Testing for Quantum Programs
description: Test your understanding of property-based testing techniques for quantum code.

questions.question: What is the key difference between property-based testing and traditional unit testing?

options.text: Property-based tests verify invariants for arbitrary generated inputs
Property-based tests only work with quantum code
Property-based tests require fewer test cases

questions.explanation: Property-based testing verifies that properties/invariants hold for randomly generated inputs, providing broader coverage than fixed test cases.

questions.question: What property must all quantum gates satisfy?
questions.explanation: All quantum gates must be unitary (U^dagger U = I), ensuring they preserve the norm of quantum states.

questions.question: Which property should all quantum observables (measurement operators) satisfy?
questions.explanation: Observables must be Hermitian (O = O^dagger) to have real eigenvalues, which represent measurable values.

questions.question: What is 'shrinking' in property-based testing?

options.text: Reducing the number of test cases
Finding a minimal failing example after a property violation

questions.explanation: Shrinking attempts to find the smallest/simplest input that still causes the property to fail, making debugging easier.

questions.question: What invariant is preserved under unitary evolution of a density matrix?
questions.explanation: Unitary evolution preserves eigenvalues, and since von Neumann entropy depends only on eigenvalues, it's also preserved.

questions.question: The tensor product distributes over matrix multiplication: (A tensor B)(C tensor D) = (AC) tensor (BD).
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental property of tensor products that enables independent operations on separate subsystems.

questions.question: Why is generating random unitary matrices using QR decomposition effective?

options.text: QR decomposition is the fastest algorithm
The Q matrix from QR decomposition is orthogonal/unitary

questions.explanation: QR decomposition of any matrix produces an orthogonal Q matrix (unitary for complex), providing a way to generate random unitaries from random matrices.
